formations_blocks: Germany building from a proactive, attacking setup with repeated long balls into advanced areas
Ecuador appearing compact and reactive in the opening exchanges, dealing with direct balls and transitions
adjustments: Germany repeatedly targeting long passes down the middle and left channel to bypass Ecuador's first line
Ecuador responding by countering quickly after turnovers, with Plata and Yeboah breaking forward on the right
key_matchups: Wirtz vs Ecuador's back line/cover down the left
Musiala vs Ordonez
Pavlovic vs Vite
Kimmich vs Plata
Pacho vs Wirtz
phases.summary: The opening phase is defined by direct German attacks, especially long balls into Wirtz and central runners, which immediately create danger and lead to Sané's early goal. Ecuador then grow into the game through transitions and pressure on loose German touches, with Angulo’s equaliser coming after Germany’s own sloppiness rather than sustained Ecuadorian control. The pattern is end-to-end rather than settled positional play, with both sides exposed when the first pass or challenge breaks down.

formations_blocks: Germany defending after an early lead, with Ecuador stepping up and going toe-to-toe
Ecuador in an aggressive pressing / front-foot attacking phase
Ecuador briefly in a more patient pass-and-probe approach around 27'
adjustments: Ecuador's response to conceding early was to increase risk and attack more directly
Ecuador alternated between direct wide attacks and a more patient circulation before the hydration break
Germany briefly re-entered the attacking phase through wider left-sided crossing
key_matchups: Hincapie vs Kimmich down Germany's left
Franco vs Germany's right side, with Kimmich involved in clearing deliveries
Plata vs Germany's left side on the Ecuador right
Sane tracking back to stop Hincapie inside the box
Wirtz's blind reverse pass intercepted by Angulo
phases.summary: After conceding early, Ecuador became the more aggressive side, pushing Germany back with direct wide attacks and sustained pressure down both flanks. Germany were largely under the cosh, with Kimmich, Sane and later Neuer repeatedly involved in defensive interventions, while their own attacking threat was limited to occasional left-sided crosses and a Havertz header. The phase ends with Ecuador still carrying the greater momentum and winning territory through Angulo's interception and forward carry after the hydration break.

adjustments: Germany make a substitution at the restart: Pavlovic is replaced by Stiller, with Pavlovic having been on a booking.
key_matchups: Thiaw vs Angulo on the left flank
phases.summary: The brief is mainly a halftime checkpoint rather than a full tactical exposition, with Germany already adjusting personnel after the interval by bringing on Stiller for booked Pavlovic. The only live phase detail after the restart is Thiaw clipping Angulo on the left, creating a dangerous free kick and suggesting that side is an immediate area of concern.

formations_blocks: Germany attacking in a fairly static structure with little penetration after the restart
Ecuador counterattacking and growing into a more lively transitional phase
Ecuador set-pieces becoming a route back into the game
adjustments: Germany substituted Undav for Havertz at 60'
Germany also replaced Kimmich with Thiaw at 60', with Kimmich passing the captain's armband to Rüdiger
Nagelsmann’s animated touchline reaction suggests an in-game tweak or dissatisfaction, though no explicit shape change is stated
key_matchups: Wirtz vs a cluster of three Ecuador defenders inside the box
Ordonez vs Havertz in the penalty-area challenge leading to the overturned penalty
Yeboah vs Germany's central and right-side defensive coverage
Yeboah and Valencia combining in Ecuador’s counters
Neuer vs Valencia on the 62' long-range effort
phases.summary: This phase is defined by Germany’s stagnation after the restart, with very little attacking threat until a substitution brings on Undav at 60'. Ecuador, by contrast, begin to find transitions and right-sided entries through Yeboah, while also threatening from set pieces and a Valencia shot that forces a good Neuer save. The only major tactical shift explicitly shown is Germany’s double change, suggesting a move to freshen the attack and re-balance the side.

formations_blocks: Ecuador defending deeper in a compact block after taking the lead
Germany forcing pressure late on, relying on crosses and set pieces
End-to-end transitional attacking phases after the 77th-minute goal
adjustments: Ecuador made a double change: Yeboah and Angulo off for Torres and Jordy Caicedo
Germany shifted emphasis toward left-sided deliveries from Raum and dead-ball pressure
key_matchups: Pacho vs Beier
Raum vs Ecuador’s back line on left-sided crosses
Plata vs Neuer on the 77th-minute corner
Beier vs Estupinan on the flank and in aerial duels
Plata vs Musiala
phases.summary: Ecuador’s 77th-minute goal flipped the phase, after which they became the more assured side, sitting deeper and looking increasingly comfortable as Germany struggled to create anything in open play. Germany’s late push came mainly through Raum’s left-sided crosses and set pieces, but Ecuador defended their box well and even threatened in transition, while Plata’s aggressive work both produced the winner and later drew a dangerous free kick the other way.
